card.intuition: Three pointers (lo, mid, hi) partition an array into three regions in a single pass: elements < pivot, = pivot, > pivot.
card.analogy: Sorting a pile of red, white, and blue balls into three sections: one worker handles the left end, one the right, and one walks through the middle assigning each ball to its zone.
when_to_use: Sort an array of exactly 3 distinct values (e.g., 0/1/2, R/G/B) in one pass
Partition step for 3-way quicksort (handles duplicates efficiently — avoids O(n²) on all-equal arrays)
Any problem where you need to group elements around a pivot into <, =, > regions
gotchas: Only increment mid after a 0-swap (lo) and for equal-to-pivot — when swapping with hi, do NOT increment mid because the swapped element is unknown
Loop condition is mid <= hi (not mid < hi) — the element at mid is unclassified until processed
This is the key partition in Dijkstra's 3-way quicksort, which is O(n log n) even on nearly-duplicate arrays

exercise.prompt: Complete the Dutch National Flag sort to arrange an array of 0s, 1s, and 2s in place.
exercise.starter_code:
def sort_colors(nums):
    lo, mid, hi = 0, 0, len(nums) - 1
    while mid <= hi:
        if nums[mid] == 0:
            nums[lo], nums[mid] = nums[mid], nums[lo]
            lo += 1
            mid += 1
        elif nums[mid] == 1:
            mid += 1
        else:  # nums[mid] == 2
            nums[mid], nums[hi] = ___________
            hi -= 1
            # Do NOT increment mid — the swapped element is unknown
    return nums
exercise.solution: nums[hi], nums[mid]     # swap mid and hi (note: args reversed to match left side)
hints: Swapping with hi brings an unknown element to mid — don't advance mid, let the next iteration classify it
The swap is: nums[mid], nums[hi] = nums[hi], nums[mid]
exercise.test_code:
assert sort_colors([2,0,2,1,1,0]) == [0,0,1,1,2,2], 'Standard case'
assert sort_colors([2,0,1])        == [0,1,2],       '3 elements'
assert sort_colors([0])             == [0],            'Single 0'
assert sort_colors([2,2,2])        == [2,2,2],        'All same'
assert sort_colors([1,0,2,1,0])    == [0,0,1,1,2],   'Mixed'
print('PASS')
